summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording of an intimate acoustic set – Bird Song, Cassidy and Monkey & the Engineer shine despite occasional noise and clipping.
review: The show captures a rare, heartfelt acoustic performance for a small group of special‑needs children.  Jerry’s vocals are surprisingly clear, the band delivers standout renditions of Bird Song, Cassidy, Ripple and Monkey & the Engineer, and the ambience of the tiny hall adds magic.  The Betty Cantor audience mic picks up most details, though listeners note occasional clipping, background noise and a need for good speakers.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive commentary on both performance (many 5‑star praises) and recording quality, with only a few isolated complaints about noise or distortion.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Cantor audience mic captures the room vividly; vocals are well‑miked despite no PA.  Listeners report occasional clipping, background noise and a need for high‑fidelity playback equipment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, intimate acoustic set for a special‑needs children’s event; warm introductions, no PA, and a relaxed vibe that lets the band dial in beautifully.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is clear and surprisingly well‑mic'd; he even talks to a child about "rock‑n‑roll".  Some note his acoustic guitar sounds slightly distorted/overdriven.
band_member_comments.Phil: Briefly quoted responding to the emcee with "Years of practice!", indicating good bass presence but no detailed critique.
band_member_comments.Bob: Only mentioned in a mispronunciation anecdote; no performance commentary.
